_comment: Per-model metadata for the /dinosaur-3d cluster photoreal-asset layer. Hand-authored; make-dinosaur-3d-manifest.mjs hashes each .glb and emits manifest.json. Every entry MUST be freely redistributable (CC0 / CC-BY / CC-BY-SA / public-domain; SA entries: the re-hosted derivative stays under the same ShareAlike license - see CREDITS.txt), verified per-file (see licenses/CREDITS.txt). 'targetLenUnits' is the scene-unit length the viewer scales the model's longest axis to (12 matches the size-vs-human reference in the page). 'kind' = fleshed | skeleton (drives the honesty disclosure).

sinosauropteryx.credit: Sinosauropteryx Figurine model by DragonArtist15 (CC-BY-SA 4.0, via Printables; STL sculpt decimated 6.5M->35k tris, ShareAlike: re-hosted derivative remains under CC-BY-SA 4.0)

denversaurus.author: Pan Wolodyjowski
denversaurus.credit: Denversaurus Schlessmani model by Pan Wolodyjowski (CC-BY 4.0, via Printables)
denversaurus.notes: dinosaur-3d-discovery-loop-runbook LEAN one-off fire (2026-07-23, second ship this session). Candidate sourced fresh via WebSearch (tier-1 nodosaur/ankylosaur family sweep for borealopelta/torosaurus/carcharodontosaurus/smilodon/megalodon all came up empty or NC-licensed - see rejected leads below) then a targeted search for 'borealopelta nodosaur printables STL' surfaced creator Pan Wolodyjowski's per-species nodosaur series (Niobrarasaurus CC-BY-NC rejected, Pawpawsaurus CC-BY-NC-ND rejected, Denversaurus CC-BY 4.0 CLEARED) - same trusted creator already vendored for estemmenosuchus/placodus/beipiaosaurus in prior fires, not on the suspected-rip list. License CONFIRMED via anonymous GraphQL query{print(id:643348){license{name}}} -> 'Creative Commons - Attribution' (no NC/SA/ND restriction). Single STL (2,357,922 verts / 4,715,840 tris, 235,792,084 bytes, byte-exact vs GraphQL stls[].fileSize), downloaded anonymously (HTTP 200, no auth) via the flat filePreviewPath-folder + lowercased-filename heuristic (media.printables.com/media/prints/643348/stls/<hash>/denversaurus.stl). REJECTED LEADS this fire (documented for the reject-trap ledger): smilodon id=1227440 by 3DPau (CC-BY-NC-ND, reject on license); smilodon id=764470 + carcharodontosaurus id=752989 both by 'seth the yutyrannus' (creator already flagged in a prior fire's rip-check sweep alongside zuniceratops - description on 764470 was the generic mismatched 'knife tooth', a rip-farm signal - skipped without further check); meglodon id=532996 by Rafae Khan (Public Domain license but summary 'remix of the articulated shark' with empty description and no stls[] returned via GraphQL - inconclusive/likely a generic shark toy remix, not a groundable Megalodon reconstruction - skipped); Land Shark Megalo Rex id=484470 + Sea of Thieves Megalodon id=303991 (both CC-BY-NC, reject on license; the latter also a video-game-IP derivative). ORIENTATION: raw STL frame is Z-up (dorsal-ventral, extent Z=31.2) with Y as the head-tail length axis (extent Y=101.5, X=34.8 width) - same layout as dunkleosteus, NOT already Y-up. Head-end determined via cross-sectional-width binning along Y (20 bins): a SHORT sharp taper from wide torso down to a point over just 15 units on the +Y side (Y=31->46.5) vs a LONG gradual taper over 46 units on the -Y side (Y=-55->-9) - matches known nodosaur proportions (short broad skull+neck vs long tapering tail) - so head/skull at Y=+46.5, tail tip at Y=-55; confirmed visually against the Printables preview thumbnail (armored skull with small pointed snout at one end, row of tail osteoderms tapering to a point at the other, legs visible below the trunk). Applied rotation (x,y,z)->(x,z,-y) [-90 deg about X] baked into the exported geometry (head ends up at -Z, tail at +Z; dorsal/armor at high-Y, legs/belly at low-Y - no inversion). Converted via Python 3 + open3d 0.19.0 - remove_duplicated_vertices/remove_degenerate_triangles/remove_unreferenced_vertices, simplify_quadric_decimation 4,715,840 -> 12,000 triangles, recentered X/Z at origin + grounded min-Y at 0 + scaled so length (Z extent) = 12 scene units, per-vertex height-based countershading (dark olive-brown armor RGB 0.20/0.22/0.14 at the dorsal/high-Y end fading to pale bony underside RGB 0.55/0.50/0.38 at the ventral/low-Y legs), binary glTF export (385,252 bytes, gltf magic verified). Render-verified via 2D axis-pair scatter projections of the final decimated+colored+scaled GLB reloaded from disk (a 3D Poly3DCollection render was tried first and produced a misleading totem-pole-looking artifact from mpl's lack of depth-sorting on a dense point cloud - discarded in favor of the more reliable 2D scatter check): side view (Z vs Y) shows a clear standing quadruped silhouette - legs planted near y=0, arched armored back up to y~3.5, head/shoulder bulge at one Z end, tail gradually tapering at the other; top view (X vs Z) shows the expected shield-shaped nodosaur silhouette, wide at the shoulders and tapering to a point at the tail tip; front view (X vs Y) shows a domed armored cross-section with two legs at the bottom. No inversion, no foreshortening, not standing on its tail. Archetype for the mandatory G16 procedural first-paint fallback: reuses the existing ankylosaur procedural body pattern (armor=true, clubTail=false since Denversaurus is a nodosaurid without a tail club, distinguishing it from the already-shipped ankylosaurus) - this glTF is the swapped-in higher-quality layer. Figures for the page cite Gregory S. Paul's 2010 estimate (~6m/20ft length, ~3 tonnes) per Wikipedia, plus the honest taxonomic note that some researchers consider Denversaurus a species of or synonymous with Edmontonia (named as a distinct genus by Bakker 1988 from a holotype originally described by Carpenter & Breithaupt 1986). WIRED to a page this same fire: full CMS/JSP/registry bundle authored + gates run + shipped to staging and mirrored to prod (see wrapper ledger for commit SHAs).

placodus.author: Pan Wolodyjowski
placodus.credit: Placodus model by Pan Wolodyjowski (CC-BY 4.0, via Printables)
placodus.notes: dinosaur-3d-discovery-loop-runbook LEAN one-off fire (2026-07-23). Backlog candidate was tier-3 pending (Triassic marine reptile / placodont). Found via printables.com/search/models?q=placodus curl with a browser User-Agent (WebFetch hit the documented 'Header overflow' error on the search page; plain curl worked). Creator 'Pan Wolodyjowski' is the same trusted uploader already vendored cleanly for estemmenosuchus/wendiceratops/pinacosaurus/panoplosaurus etc - not on the suspected-rip list; model id 664415 is numerically adjacent to the already-verified estemmenosuchus id 664469 (same upload batch of Permian/Triassic creatures), title 'Placodus' with no franchise/game tokens, category Animals. License confirmed via anonymous GraphQL query{print(id:664415){license{id name}}} -> id 1 = 'Creative Commons - Attribution' (no NC/SA restriction). Single binary STL (4,055,104 triangles, 202,755,284 bytes, byte-exact vs GraphQL fileSize), downloaded anonymously via the flat filePreviewPath-folder + lowercased-filename heuristic (media.printables.com/media/prints/664415/stls/<hash>/placodus.stl, HTTP 200). ORIENTATION: raw STL is ALREADY correctly oriented with no rotation needed - confirmed quantitatively via a bilateral-mirror nearest-neighbor test (mirroring the point cloud across the STL X-axis gave near-zero mean displacement (0.16 units) vs Y-axis (3.2) and Z-axis (8.5), proving X is the left-right symmetry axis, Y is dorsal-ventral/up (belly+legs at low-Y -31..0, back at high-Y up to +7.7), and Z is the head-tail length axis (extent 128, by far the largest)) AND visually via a full shaded-triangle render with the STL's own Y mapped to the vertical screen axis, showing a correctly-posed quadruped: head with visible eye socket/mouth at one Z end, four legs planted below (low Y), tail tapering at the other Z end - feet down, dorsal up, no inversion, no foreshortening. NO pitch/yaw correction applied. Converted via Python 3 + open3d 0.19.0 + trimesh 4.12.2 (Blender unavailable/hangs on this machine, matching prior fires' finding; open3d's simplify_quadric_decimation produced holed/degenerate output on this particular 4M-triangle mesh at multiple target ratios - open3d's simplify_vertex_clustering (voxel size = bbox diagonal/60) succeeded cleanly at 6,081 triangles in ~26s) - then trimesh for recenter X/Z at origin + ground min-Y at 0 + scale so length (Z extent) = 12 scene units + per-vertex height-based countershading (dark warm brown RGB 77/51/33 at the dorsal/high-Y end fading to pale tan RGB 158/128/87 at the ventral/low-Y legs, matching the estemmenosuchus precedent convention) + binary glTF export (117,448 bytes, gltf magic verified). Render-verified a second time on the final decimated+colored+scaled GLB (reloaded from disk): clean, correctly-proportioned, unmistakable stocky placodont silhouette - flattened turtle-like body, four legs planted, tapering tail, countershading correct, feet at y=0, not inverted. glb-orientation-audit.py clean (no WRONG-UP flag). Archetype for the mandatory G16 procedural first-paint fallback: marine-reptile archetype (aquatic=false at rest on a rocky shoreline - placodonts foraged in shallow coastal waters, not open ocean like mosasaurs/ichthyosaurs - so the viewer omits the flippers/tailFluke/water-plane feats used by mosasaurus and instead uses a squat quadrupedal stance with a crushing-tooth jaw detail) - this glTF is the swapped-in higher-quality layer. WIRED to a page this same fire: full CMS/JSP/registry bundle authored + gates run + shipped to staging and mirrored to prod (see wrapper ledger for commit SHAs).

wendiceratops.author: Pan Wolodyjowski
wendiceratops.credit: Wendiceratops model by Pan Wolodyjowski (CC-BY 4.0, via Printables)
wendiceratops.notes: dino3d-discovery-loop-runbook fire237 (2026-07-21, LEAN one-off, curate-only fire - sibling dinosaur-3d-guides-loop confirmed live via ps -ef (pid 59350/59356 _run-fires-229-243.mjs, elapsed ~2h, mid fire242 with an active laneGit.mjs staging push+wait-for-deploy in progress at fire start), so per the fire228-236 precedent zero writes were made to freetoolonline-web/-test; this vendoring targets only the independent ftol-vm-assets CDN repo). Resolves the fire234 MODEL_LEAD_FOUND_DOWNLOAD_UNRESOLVED verdict (same Printables 651498 lead, license id 1 = Creative Commons - Attribution, uploader Pan Wolodyjowski NOT on the suspected-rip list; two later revisions by the same uploader, 927915 and 1139466, remain non-admissible CC-BY-NC / CC-BY-NC-ND and were NOT used). NEW technique this fire (simpler than the fire225 getDownloadLink GraphQL mutation): GraphQL `print(id:651498){ stls{ id name fileSize filePreviewPath } }` returns each STL's display name ("Wendiceratops.stl") and its filePreviewPath folder token; the actual STL sits in that SAME folder at the LOWERCASED display name ("wendiceratops.stl") on media.printables.com (a GCS-backed bucket) - HTTP 200, content-length byte-exact (86,096,084) against the GraphQL fileSize. Header bytes all-zero (no Aspose/Daz exporter signature); Blender 'Separate by Loose Parts' confirmed a single connected island (860,962 verts / 1,721,920 tris, no print-support plinth to strip). ORIENTATION DEFECT FOUND+FIXED: the raw STL is print-posed standing UPRIGHT along its own spine axis (legs splayed symmetrically to the sides as print supports) rather than lying naturally on 4 legs - confirmed via 5 diagnostic renders (2 perspective angles unambiguously showed a vertical hanging pose: frilled/horned head up, tail down, legs splayed left-right). Fix: rotate +90deg about the raw X axis (leaves raw-X/width untouched, swaps raw-Y/raw-Z between depth/height), recenter X/Y at origin, ground at Z=0 - the FIRST candidate rotation tried rendered correctly on the first attempt (feet planted, shadow cast, head/frill forward, tail trailing back, no inversion), verified against a rendered ground-plane 3/4-perspective reference before finalizing. Converted via real Blender 4.5.11 headless (wm.stl_import, rotate+transform_apply, remove_doubles weld, vertex-color countershading dark-olive-back RGB 0.22/0.26/0.13 to pale-tan-belly RGB 0.74/0.66/0.48 by post-rotation height, quadric decimate 1,721,920 -> 9,000 tris, GLB export Y-up with export_vertex_color='MATERIAL' + export_all_vertex_colors=True, no Draco - site GLTFLoader has no DRACOLoader wired). Render-verified twice independently on the final decimated+colored GLB (a ground-plane 3/4 perspective render and a top-down orthographic render): clean, correctly-proportioned centrosaurine ceratopsian silhouette - nose horn, hook-horned frill, four legs planted, tapering tail, countershading applied correctly, no leftover artifacts. NOT YET WIRED to a page this fire - added to _model_available_backlog.ready (6th entry) in the wrapper ledger for the next fire that finds the lock + sibling genuinely free.

pinacosaurus.author: Pan Wolodyjowski
pinacosaurus.credit: Pinacosaurus model by Pan Wolodyjowski (CC-BY 4.0, via Printables)
pinacosaurus.notes: dino3d-discovery-loop-runbook fire236 (2026-07-21, LEAN one-off, curate-only fire - sibling dinosaur-3d-guides-loop confirmed live via ps -ef laneGit.mjs+wait-for-deploy processes mid-push, so per the fire228-235 precedent zero writes were made to freetoolonline-web/-test; this vendoring targets only the independent ftol-vm-assets CDN repo). NEW species not previously in the candidate_backlog: found via a fresh printables.com/search/models?q=pinacosaurus curl (WebFetch hit the same 'Header overflow' error documented for fire233/126/133; plain curl with a browser User-Agent worked). Same trusted 'Pan Wolodyjowski' creator already vendored cleanly for panoplosaurus - no rip-signature concerns. License confirmed via anonymous GraphQL query{print(id:643281){license{name}}} -> 'Creative Commons - Attribution'. Binary STL (4,453,696 triangles, 222,684,884 bytes, byte-exact vs GraphQL fileSize); header bytes all-zero (no Aspose/Daz exporter signature). Single connected mesh (verified via Blender 'Separate by Loose Parts' -> 1 island, no separate print-support plinth). ORIENTATION FIX (the real work this fire): the raw STL is print-posed standing UPRIGHT along its own long axis (a common support-minimizing print orientation for elongated miniatures), NOT lying naturally on 4 legs - naive axis-aligned diagnostic renders (looking down each of the 3 raw axes) initially looked like a bizarre vertical totem; resolved by rendering 8 azimuthal 3/4 perspective views orbiting the raw model, which revealed a perfectly recognizable ankylosaur when viewed from the side of its 'standing' pose. Fix: rotate +90 deg about the raw Y axis (leaves the raw Y/width axis untouched, swaps raw-X/raw-Z between height/length), then recenter X/Y at origin and ground at Z=0; verified against a rendered ground-plane reference (feet planted, shadow cast, back-up/belly-down, not inverted) before finalizing. Converted via real Blender 4.5.11 headless (wm.stl_import, rotate+transform_apply, vertex-color countershading dark-olive-back RGB 0.24/0.27/0.16 to pale-tan-belly RGB 0.72/0.63/0.46 by post-rotation height, quadric decimate 4,453,696 -> 9,000 tris, GLB export Y-up with export_vertex_color='MATERIAL', no Draco - site GLTFLoader has no DRACOLoader wired). Render-verified twice independently (a ground-plane 3/4 perspective render and a top-down orthographic render on the final decimated+colored GLB): clean, correctly-proportioned, low-slung armored ankylosaur silhouette, four legs planted, tail with terminal club, back studs/spikes visible, countershading applied correctly (darker back, paler underside). NOT YET WIRED to a page this fire (model-sourcing + vendoring only, per the same time-boxing as fire228/229/232/233) - added directly to _model_available_backlog.ready (5th entry) in the wrapper ledger for the next fire that finds the lock + sibling genuinely free.

becklespinax.author: seth the yutyrannus
becklespinax.credit: Becklespinax model by seth the yutyrannus (CC-BY-SA 4.0, via Printables)
becklespinax.notes: dino3d-discovery-loop-runbook fire225 (2026-07-20). Model-download-byte resolution BREAKTHROUGH this fire: the long-standing filePreviewPath-folder-hash heuristic (blocking becklespinax/agujaceratops/deltadromeus/wuerhosaurus/dracorex/microceratus across fires 216-220) is REPLACED by the actual Printables production mechanism - the getDownloadLink GraphQL mutation (id=<stl_id>, printId=<print_id>, fileType='stl', source='model_detail') against api.printables.com/graphql/, confirmed anonymous, mints a fresh TTL-bound (86400s) signed URL for ANY print/file (not just flat-layout ones) - filePreviewPath was a red herring the whole time. Downloaded becklespinax.obj (800,686 bytes, byte-exact vs GraphQL fileSize; Blender 3.6.17 export; single object 'Brunette_Brunette_0', 5,757 verts / 9,606 faces). Rip-check: grepped for Daz/Dinoraul/Jurassic/franchise/copyright/trademark/Sammy/Citipati strings - none found, clean (contrast: this SAME technique applied to agujaceratops and deltadromeus this fire surfaced origin filenames literally named 'lmx3d_daz_originals__dino_rauls_agujaceratops.obj' and 'daz_originals__dinorauls_deltadromeus.obj' - both REJECTED as Daz-Originals/Dino-Raul commercial re-uploads mislabeled CC-BY-SA; see candidate_backlog for those 2 slugs). Converted via trimesh (OBJ load, -90deg rotation about Y to bring the long Z-axis into X per convention, center X/Z at origin, feet translated to Y=0, neutral olive-tan vertex-color applied since no skin/color evidence survives a 3-vertebrae holotype) - no decimation needed (already under the ~20k tri convention at 9,606 faces). Final GLB: 208,456 bytes, extents X=15.46/Y=6.31/Z=2.91 scene units pre-normalize (loader scales longest axis to targetLenUnits=12 at runtime). Species note for the page author: Becklespinax is known ONLY from 3 fused dorsal vertebrae (originally referred to Altispinax) - gate 1 content verdict is ELIGIBLE_WITH_CAUTION, any BODYWELCOME copy MUST disclose the fragmentary holotype explicitly and frame length/mass as composite genus-level estimates, not as directly-measured figures. Vendored to the ready queue; no page/CMS/registry authored this fire (time-boxed to model-sourcing + vendoring).

kentrosaurus.author: seth the yutyrannus
kentrosaurus.credit: Kentrosaurus model by seth the yutyrannus (CC-BY-SA 4.0, via Printables; STL->glTF conversion with applied countershading)
kentrosaurus.notes: dino3d-discovery-loop-runbook fire (2026-07-20). Lead first found fire139, flagged NEEDS_VERIFICATION because the same uploader had a 2-of-7 Daz-rip rate on this loop's checks and the binary STL carries no object/material names to run the usual Daz-tell check. Re-verified this fire: downloaded kentrosaurus.stl (12,716,484 bytes, 127,162 verts / 254,328 faces, single watertight connected component - not a multi-part Daz-rip assembly). Geometry sanity-checked via a matplotlib depth-sorted shaded wireframe (both sides): unmistakable quadrupedal stegosaur silhouette - grazing head-down pose, 4 sturdy clawed legs, alternating plates-to-spikes row rising from the neck and peaking mid-back before tapering into long paired tail spikes (matches the published 'at least 7 pairs of tail spikes' distinguishing anatomy) - not a generic/fantasy stand-in. Converted headlessly via Blender 4.5 bpy (STL import -> join -> recalculate normals -> Decimate modifier ratio-applied to bring 254,328 faces down to 20,000 for web-viewer weight -> glTF export), then trimesh (recentered X/Z at 0, feet at Y=0, vertex-color countershading applied: dark olive-brown back/plates to pale cream belly, a plausible stegosaur-family palette since no skin/color evidence survives in the fossil record) - no baked texture in the source STL. Final GLB: 59,992 verts / 20,000 faces, 1,200,904 bytes. Render-verified post-conversion via a second matplotlib pass on the final colored GLB: shape and countershading intact, no inverted normals or holes introduced by decimation. ShareAlike: re-hosted derivative remains under CC-BY-SA 4.0.

pentaceratops.author: Manicaeks
pentaceratops.credit: Pentaceratops model by Manicaeks (CC0, via OpenGameArt)
pentaceratops.notes: OpenGameArt submission licensed CC0 (Public Domain), by the same author as the already-vendored albertosaurus/ankylosaurus models. Zip held only .blend (Blender 2.69) + .fbx (too old for assimp) + 2 texture PNGs (a full-res 1024x1024 painted color texture + a UV-layout reference). Converted headlessly via Blender 4.5 bpy: legacy use_nodes=False material rebuilt as a Principled BSDF wired to the real painted texture via a UVMap node (same technique as albertosaurus); texture re-encoded to JPEG q82 on glTF export to shrink the asset from 3.2MB to ~120KB with no visible quality loss. Render-verified via a Blender headless Eevee snapshot: clear quadruped ceratopsian - frill with epoccipital spikes, nose horn + 2 brow horns, painted striped purple skin, digitigrade legs with claws. No Daz/Dinoraul/franchise/toy-scan markers (independent hand-modeled 2014 asset). Ready-queue entry per dino3d-discovery-loop-runbook fire123 - no page authored this fire; vendored ahead of need for a future fire to build.

albertosaurus.author: Manicaeks
albertosaurus.credit: Albertosaurus model by Manicaeks (CC0, via OpenGameArt)
albertosaurus.notes: OpenGameArt submission licensed CC0 (Public Domain), part 3 of the author's 'dinosaurs pack' (sibling ankylosaurus already on this manifest, same author; apatosaurus already on this manifest from a different creator, Quaternius; pentaceratops, also by this author, queued for a future fire). Zip contained only .blend (Blender 2.69) + .fbx (ASCII FBX 6.1.0, too old for assimp) - converted headlessly via Blender 4.5 bpy (--background --python), object named 'Cube' 343 polys/1364 tris. Legacy material had use_nodes=False (Blender-Internal era) with no node-graph diffuse color, so a Principled BSDF was rebuilt from scratch wired to the model's own painted UV texture (albertosaurustexture.png, 1024x1024, via a UVMap node) rather than the flat gray diffuse_color fallback. Render-verified via a Blender headless snapshot (Eevee, sun lighting): clear bipedal tyrannosaurid silhouette - horizontal back, raised tail counterweight, big head with visible eye+jaw, small tucked arms, digitigrade legs. No Daz/Dinoraul/franchise/toy-scan markers (independent hand-modeled asset, 2014).

chasmosaurus.notes: dino3d-discovery-loop-runbook fire149. Printables model 1766773, Model Magic 'Dinosaur Art - Chasmosaurus', license CC0 / Public Domain (Printables license id 7). barosaurus.notes: Printables model 885614, license CC-BY-SA 4.0 (Attribution-ShareAlike) confirmed on the live model page (Creative Commons 4.0 International License badge, Commercial Use/Remix Culture/Free Cultural Works all green); Model origin states "The author marked this model as their own original creation" - no franchise/toy-scan markers, not a game-asset rip. Single OBJ file (21534_Barosaurus_v1, 47386 verts, 3ds Max Wavefront exporter, no accompanying MTL/texture downloaded) resolved via the real browser Download button on the page Files tab (Playwright page.waitForEvent("download") while clicking the row-level Download control - NOT the GraphQL API, which stayed introspection-disabled and unresolvable for this creators tyrannotitan/euoplocephalus leads in fires 129/132; this technique is the fire134 breakthrough and should be reused directly for those two backlog leads next). Converted headlessly via trimesh: loaded OBJ, remapped the source Z-up (3ds Max) axes to glTF Y-up (newX=oldX, newY=oldZ, newZ=-oldY - confirmed necessary by a before/after Playwright swiftshader render showing the pre-fix export standing on its tail end), applied a vertex-color countershading gradient (light tan belly to dark olive-brown back) since the source OBJ carried no baked texture/material, re-centered on X/Z with feet at Y=0, exported as GLB (49856 verts post-trimesh dedup, 94768 faces, 1.9MB). targetLenUnits set to 25 to match the mamenchisaurus longNeck-sauropod camera/ground framing precedent (feats.longNeck:true uses the same procedural camera radius/ground-disk tuned for a ~25-unit tail-to-head span, not the 12-unit default used by compact archetypes). Render-verified via Playwright + the sites own vendored three.module.min.js/gltf-loader.module.js: clear quadruped sauropod silhouette - long neck raised, barrel body, four legs, long tapering tail.

Same fire also downloaded and REJECTED two Daz-rip sibling leads from the SAME creator: euoplocephalus (Printables 1023372, filename literally 'daz_originals__dinorauls_euoplocephalus.obj', Object_5-10 groups, generic claws/horn/mouth/skin/teeth materials) and suchomimus (Printables 747445, same Object_5-11 + claws/horns/material/mouth/skin/teeth/tongue material pattern) - do not resume either. This Torvosaurus OBJ: Aspose.3D Wavefront exporter, g Object_7/9/11, single usemtl 'Torvosaurus' (species-named, not generic anatomical zones) - byte-for-byte the SAME object-count/material-naming pattern as the already-vendored, confirmed-clean Deinocheirus (Object_7/9/11, usemtl Deinocheirus), independently re-verified this fire by re-downloading Deinocheirus's original OBJ and diffing its material block. 62,694 raw verts / 95,120 faces at print scale (extents ~0.014 x 0.033 x 0.118, i.e. authored directly at physical print size, same as every other seth-the-yutyrannus source); recentered X/Z, feet at Y=0, no axis swap needed (native X=width/Y=height/Z=length already matches the shipped Deinocheirus's identical native axis ordering, confirmed by comparing raw bounding boxes before assuming a Barosaurus-style Z-up fix was required); scaled so length(Z) maps to targetLenUnits 14 (matches the shipped Albertosaurus/Tyrannosaurus large-theropod framing, not the 12-unit default); vertex-color countershading (cream belly to dark brown back), no baked texture in the source. Post-merge 48,640 verts / 95,120 faces, 1.9MB. Render-verified via a matplotlib side/top wireframe projection AND via the live page (Playwright + system Chrome + swiftshader flags DOES render WebGL headless in this environment once pointed at the system Chrome.app binary - corrects a prior assumption in this fire's own working notes that only page/DOM automation worked, not GPU compositing) - clear bipedal theropod silhouette: head with open jaws at the forward end, raised tail counterweight, digitigrade legs, back arched over the hips, tail lifted off the ground. ShareAlike: re-hosted derivative remains under CC-BY-SA 4.0.

sarcosuchus.notes: dino3d-discovery-loop-runbook fire144. License CC-BY-SA 4.0 GraphQL-confirmed; no Daz/franchise naming marker; same trusted uploader as the already-vendored barosaurus/torvosaurus/deinocheirus/troodon/ostafrikasaurus. Geometry sanity-checked fire143 via offline matplotlib wireframe on the raw OBJ: unmistakable long tapered crocodilian snout with an open toothy jaw, dorsal osteoderm ridge along the back, low-slung body, tapering tail, 4 legs correctly sprawled (crocodilian gait). sarcosuchus_no_animated.obj: 23804 verts / 42728 faces, native axes already Y-up/length-along-Z (no axis swap needed, same as torvosaurus/deinocheirus). Converted headlessly via trimesh: recentered X/Z, feet at Y=0, scaled so length(Z) maps to targetLenUnits 14 (matches the torvosaurus large-predator framing, appropriate for Sarcosuchus's 9.5-11.65m published length); applied vertex-color countershading (olive-green back to cream belly, real crocodilian palette) since the source OBJ carried no baked texture. Post-scale extents length:width 4.29 / length:height 5.8 - matches the pre-conversion raw-OBJ bbox ratios exactly, confirming the scale-only transform preserved proportions. Render-verified via a matplotlib side-view projection of the final colored GLB: clear crocodilian silhouette (long snout, visible teeth, dorsal scute ridge, tapering tail).

lambeosaurus.credit: Lambeosaurus model by Model Magic (CC0 1.0 Public Domain, via Printables)
lambeosaurus.notes: dinosaur-3d-discovery-loop-runbook fire228 (2026-07-20). Fresh model lead surfaced by enumerating the Model Magic creator's full 'dinosaur-art-*' series search results page directly (printables.com/search/all-models?q=dinosaur-art) rather than blind per-species search - this listing (Printables 1759419) is a DIFFERENT print than the earlier-rejected NC-licensed lambeosaurus lead (Printables 981532, rejected fire139 for CC-BY-NC). License CC0/Public Domain confirmed via GraphQL license{name}. Download resolved via the getDownloadLink GraphQL mutation (fire225 breakthrough technique): id=7326530 (stl file id), printId=1759419, fileType=stl, source=model_detail -> signed URL, byte-verified exact match against reported fileSize (51,472,484 bytes). Binary STL (no material names to rip-check; grep for daz/dinoraul/jurassic/franchise/turbosquid/cgtrader substrings clean). Converted headlessly (trimesh + Open3D): flat display-base plinth stripped via a horizontal plane cut at z=2.9 (established footprint-analysis technique - base spans x-width ~172 vs the figure's own ~56, transition confirmed by sampling per-slice cross-sections), quadric decimation 978,157 -> 16,000 faces (8,063 verts), Z-up (STL) -> Y-up (glTF) axis correction, recentered X/Z at origin with feet at Y=0, vertex-color countershading applied (dark olive-brown back to pale cream belly, plausible hadrosaur-family palette; no skin/color evidence survives in the fossil record). Render-verified via an offline matplotlib side-profile scatter plot before vendoring: unmistakable duckbill-hadrosaur silhouette on all fours with a small hollow crest bump atop the head and a long balancing tail - matches the published hatchet-shaped-crest anatomy, not a generic/mislabeled stand-in (contrast: the fire149 Corythosaurus lead from this SAME creator/series was correctly rejected as a mislabeled tube-crested Parasaurolophus preview). Final GLB: 321,992 bytes. This exact search-page-enumeration technique also surfaced a second fresh, license-clean, download-resolved lead this fire - archaeopteryx (Printables 1772799, CC0/PD, STL byte-verified 49,646,084 bytes) - queued in the ready backlog for the next fire (not shipped this fire; one species per fire per doctrine).

monolophosaurus.author: seth the yutyrannus
monolophosaurus.credit: Monolophosaurus model by seth the yutyrannus (CC-BY-SA 4.0, via Printables)
monolophosaurus.notes: dino3d-discovery-loop-runbook fire229 (2026-07-20, LEAN one-off, curate/model-sourcing while the shared cleanup-backlog lock was held live by a sibling loop). Monolophosaurus was a brand-new Sec.2a candidate this fire (not previously in the ledger's candidate_backlog) - curated + gate-checked (real Zhao & Currie 1993/1994 taxon, Shishugou Formation Xinjiang China, ~5-5.5m/475kg/163-161Mya Bathonian-Callovian, single pneumatic nasal-to-eye crest; distinct from the already-shipped Cryolophosaurus's transverse pompadour crest). Poly Pizza search returned zero species-specific match (only the standard generic Quaternius/Poly-by-Google/fantasy fallback). Printables site-search surfaced /model/764471-monolophosaurus by seth the yutyrannus (this project's established trusted uploader) - GraphQL print(id:764471) confirmed license id 2 = CC-BY-SA anonymously, and (unlike the styracosaurus/metriacanthosaurus/saurosuchus leads stuck since fire216/217) this print's filePreviewPath uses the LEGACY nested stls/<folder>/ layout, so the folder token was directly readable and the files.printables.com origin_download URL resolved on the first HEAD request (byte-exact vs GraphQL fileSize 8,668,322). Single object monolophosaurus.obj, 7 mesh components (head/eye/teeth detail meshes + main body + a thin stylised dorsal ridge decoration + hands + feet), no Daz/Dinoraul/franchise rip-marker filename. Rip-check clean. Converted via trimesh: merged all 7 components (all anatomically legitimate - no print-support base plate found), +90deg rotation about Y to bring the long native-Z axis into X per convention (proper rotation, normals verified intact - confirmed via a rendered point-cloud check, head ends up at +X), centered X/Z at origin, feet translated to Y=0. Quadric-decimated via Open3D from 76,376 to 21,999 triangles (matches this project's ~20k-tri convention); cleaned degenerate/duplicate geometry post-decimation; re-verified the silhouette still reads correctly as a bipedal theropod after decimation (rendered point-cloud comparison, no visible holes/artifacts). Final GLB 410,504 bytes, sha256 d7f7e4f0b9c07d3615bd5fb55dbe7a27d6bae3176ca62946d4a96e5a4b24100f. Vendored to the ready queue (_model_available_backlog.ready in the wrapper ledger) - no page/CMS/registry authored this fire (the shared cleanup-backlog lock covering freetoolonline-web/-web-test was held live all fire by sibling game-discovery-loop + a live dinosaur-3d-guides-loop staging push; this vendoring step touches only this separate ftol-vm-assets repo, so it proceeded safely). panoplosaurus.notes: dino3d-discovery-loop-runbook fire232 (2026-07-20/21, LEAN one-off). RESOLVES the fire216/217/231 flat-preview-bucket misdiagnosis: this print IS the legacy nested filePreviewPath layout (folder token present), but the STL internal preview stem ("yourmesh-3") does not match the display filename ("Panoplosaurus"), so the fire231 attempt at <folder>/Panoplosaurus.obj 404d. Fix: derive the real filename by stripping "_preview.png" off the basename of the GraphQL stls[].filePreviewPath field (yielding "yourmesh-3") and pairing that stem with the extension from stls[].name (.obj) -> <folder>/yourmesh-3.obj, HTTP 200, byte-exact (69,606,020 bytes) match to the GraphQL-reported fileSize. Converted via Blender 4.5 headless: wm.obj_import (default OBJ-standard Y-up axis conversion), transform_apply baked rotation into mesh data, recentered X/Z + ground-planed Y=0, vertex-color countershading (dark olive back RGB 0.20/0.19/0.12, pale tan belly RGB 0.62/0.55/0.40 by local-Z height), quadric decimate 1,178,960 -> 9,000 tris, exported glTF Y-up (NO Draco compression - the site vendored GLTFLoader has no DRACOLoader wired via setDRACOLoader(), so a Draco-compressed asset would silently fail to parse and the page would stay procedural forever). Attribution: plain CC-BY (not NC/ND/SA) confirmed via anonymous GraphQL query{print(id:643341){license{id name}}} -> id 1 "Creative Commons - Attribution". suchomimus.author: seth the yutyrannus
suchomimus.credit: Suchomimus model by seth the yutyrannus (CC-BY-SA 4.0, via Printables)
suchomimus.notes: dino3d-discovery-loop-runbook LEAN one-off fire (2026-07-21, curate + model-sourcing). Suchomimus was a Sec.2a candidate flagged with a CAUTION (close relative of the already-shipped baryonyx and spinosaurus, needs careful build-time differentiation - real distinguishing anatomy: low/short dorsal spines vs Spinosaurus's tall sail, Elrhaz Formation Niger locality vs Baryonyx's Wealden Formation England, larger size ~9.5-11m than Baryonyx's ~7.5-10m). Same trusted uploader ('seth the yutyrannus') already vendored cleanly for monolophosaurus - no rip-signature concerns (checked for daz/dinoraul/jurassic/universal/frontier tokens in the raw OBJ, none found; exporter signature is generic 'Aspose.3D Wavefront OBJ Exporter'). Printables id 747445 uses the LEGACY nested stls/<folder>/ layout so the origin_download OBJ URL resolved on the first request, byte-exact vs GraphQL fileSize (8,420,489 bytes). License confirmed via public search + item page fetch: CC-BY-SA (Printables license id 2), NOT the two other Suchomimus prints found (id 726201 CC-BY-NC-SA, id 743137 CC-BY-NC - both rejected on license). Single OBJ, 31,634 verts / 57,192 tris, one connected mesh (no separate print-support plinth token in the geometry). Raw file is print-posed standing on its tail tip (a support-minimizing orientation, confirmed via the cover photo showing a vertical acrobatic pose) but the actual mesh geometry itself already reads as a natural bipedal walking stride once correctly axis-mapped - no additional pose-fix needed beyond axis alignment. ORIENTATION: diagnosed via 6 axis-aligned renders (not the vertical cover-photo pose) - native Blender-Y was the long head-to-tail axis (14.5 units) and Blender-Z was already height (5.13 units, correctly vertical per Blender's OBJ-import Y-up-to-Z-up convention) and Blender-X was body width (2.39 units). Applied a direct-coordinate axis swap (new_x=old_y, new_y=-old_x, z unchanged) baked into mesh data (not an object-level euler rotation, which produced an incorrect compound transform on the first attempt), verified numerically (vertex-density cross-section: 4340 verts within 5% of the head end vs 614 near the thin tail tip) then flipped X sign so the head lands at +X per project convention. Converted via Blender 4.5.11 headless: axis-swap + flip baked directly into mesh.vertices, recentered X/Y at origin + grounded Z=0, quadric decimate 57,192 -> 20,000 tris (mesh already modest size, mild reduction only), vertex-color countershading dark-olive-back (0.20/0.22/0.09) to pale-tan-belly (0.70/0.62/0.40) by height, GLB export Y-up (export_apply=True), no Draco (site GLTFLoader has no DRACOLoader wired). Render-verified via re-imported GLB from 4 angles (side/top/front): clean recognizable spinosaurid silhouette - crocodile-like snout with open jaws, LOW dorsal spine row along back and tail (correctly distinct from Spinosaurus's tall sail), two legs in a walking stride, tail tapering to a point, no visible holes or decimation artifacts. Final GLB 667,408 bytes, sha256 cf2bfdcd14b084891bbad78bf999890199a8084d44ca9dfaf4c3b947616525ef. Vendored to the ready queue (_model_available_backlog.ready in the wrapper ledger) - no page/CMS/registry authored this fire (per the established fire228-236 precedent of splitting model-sourcing from page-building; the shared cleanup-backlog lock was anchored for this fire's own curate+vendor work only, this ftol-vm-assets repo push is independent of the site child repos). Also curated huayangosaurus (real Dong 1982 stegosaur, ~4-4.5m/500-1360kg depending on source [Wikipedia most-cited 4m/500kg vs AMNH 4m/1360kg - cite as a range], 165 Mya Bathonian-Callovian, Sichuan China, one of the earliest known stegosaurs, thagomizer tail spikes - distinct from the already-shipped stegosaurus/kentrosaurus by being markedly smaller/earlier/more primitive; zero CC0/CC-BY model found on Poly Pizza/OpenGameArt/Printables this fire, only paid STL-print listings with '0 free models' - recorded ELIGIBLE, procedural-only until a model surfaces) and eoraptor (real Sereno et al. 1993 theropod, ~1-1.7m/3.5-10kg depending on source [AMNH 3.5kg vs most other sources ~10kg - cite as a range], 231.4 Mya Late Triassic, Ischigualasto Formation Argentina, one of the earliest known dinosaurs, omnivorous mixed dentition - distinct from every shipped raptor/theropod by being a basal dinosauriform far outside Jurassic/Cretaceous theropod anatomy; zero model found on any of the 3 sources this fire, Printables search matched only unrelated 'raptor' (F-22 jet, claw props) - recorded ELIGIBLE, procedural-only). Also re-confirmed the archaeopteryx model-search gap has a genuine Public-Domain-licensed full-body candidate (Printables id 1772799 'Dinosaur Art - Archaeopteryx' by Model Magic, license id 7 = Public Domain, beautiful feathered full-body sculpture confirmed via the cover render) but it uses Printables' NEWER per-file UUID storage layout (not the legacy nested stls/<folder>/ layout), so no direct/guessable download URL could be resolved via the public GraphQL API this fire (same stuck pattern as the styracosaurus/metriacanthosaurus/saurosuchus leads since fire216/217) - REVIEW-only/operator-fetch or a future fire with working Playwright browser automation to capture the actual signed download URL from the Download button's network request.

gorgonops.notes: dinosaur-3d-discovery-loop-runbook LEAN fire (2026-07-22/23): curate-then-vendor. License confirmed via anonymous Printables GraphQL query{print(id:761847){license{name}}} -> literal text "Creative Commons - Attribution - Share Alike" (id=2, matches the existing becklespinax/kentrosaurus/allosaurus/dilophosaurus/gallimimus/pachycephalosaurus id=2 CC-BY-SA precedent). Source file is primeval_gorgonopsid.obj (Aspose.3D-exported Wavefront OBJ, 5,230,806 bytes, byte-exact vs GraphQL fileSize, anonymous curl HTTP 200 from the media.printables.com GCS bucket, access-control-allow-origin:*). Blender is UNVIABLE on this machine this session (headless --background --version hangs at 0% CPU indefinitely, reconfirming the exact styracosaurus finding from fire-lean-2026-07-22d) - converted instead via a Python 3.12 venv + trimesh 4.12.2 + fast-simplification (no Blender dependency). ORIENTATION: raw OBJ is print-posed standing UPRIGHT along its own spine axis (extents X=1.99/Y=3.30/Z=8.92, Z=spine/length, head at high Z with open jaw+teeth, tail tapering to a point at low Z, all 4 legs splayed to the sides as print supports) - matplotlib Poly3DCollection perspective renders (elev20/azim30) confirmed this unambiguously (visible open-mouth head at one end, curling tail at the other, 4 clawed limbs splayed). Centered X/Z at origin, grounded min-Y at 0, decimated 34,787 -> 9,000 triangles via trimesh.simplify_quadric_decimation (fast_simplification backend), scaled so length (X extent) = 12 scene units matching the manifest targetLenUnits convention, applied per-vertex countershading (dark olive-brown back RGB 0.20/0.19/0.14 to pale-tan belly RGB 0.68/0.58/0.42 by post-rotation height) via trimesh.visual.ColorVisuals, exported binary glTF (191,348 bytes, gltF magic bytes verified). Archetype: reuses the existing dimetrodon-pattern low-slung quadruped procedural body (sail-less variant, tinyArms=false) for the mandatory G16 procedural first-paint fallback; this glTF is the swapped-in higher-quality layer per the model-first render-quality mandate. styracosaurus.author: seth the yutyrannus
styracosaurus.credit: Styracosaurus model by seth the yutyrannus (CC-BY-SA 4.0, via Printables; re-hosted derivative stays under the same ShareAlike license)
styracosaurus.notes: dinosaur-3d-discovery-loop-runbook LEAN fire (2026-07-22/23, this fire): completed a candidate first curated fire-lean-2026-07-22c (license CC-BY-SA 4.0 / id=2 cross-referenced against becklespinax+kentrosaurus, both id=2) but blocked on conversion because headless Blender was unavailable on 2 prior machines (env /opt/anaconda3/envs/bpy311 absent; separately, `blender --background --version`/`--python-expr` HANGS indefinitely at 0% CPU on a machine WITH blender installed - re-confirmed a THIRD time on THIS machine via a perl fork+sleep20+kill9 probe, so this is a durable cross-machine Blender infra defect, not a one-off). BREAKTHROUGH this fire: discovered the actual file is a Wavefront OBJ (Aspose.3D exporter), not an STL despite the GraphQL STLType typename, and found its direct anonymous download URL by pattern-matching the SAME uuid-pair folder as the GraphQL-supplied `previewFile.filePreviewPath` (which only serves a PNG preview) with the real filename from `print(id){stls{name}}` (`styracosaurus.obj`) - HTTP 200, byte-exact (3,496,550 bytes) vs GraphQL fileSize, from the media.printables.com GCS bucket, access-control-allow-origin:*, NO getDownloadLink mutation needed (that mutation, documented in the becklespinax entry, is the more robust general method - this simpler heuristic happened to work here too). Converted via plain Python 3.9 + trimesh 4.12.2 (no Blender dependency, matching the dakotaraptor/gorgonops/woolly-mammoth precedent on this same infra defect): loaded OBJ (13,705 verts / 23,538 faces, no decimation needed - already within the ~20-25k tri convention), no mtllib present (single bare `usemtl TexMap.003` with no accompanying .mtl - the source has no baked texture/color at all). ORIENTATION SELF-CORRECTION (v2, same fire): an initial pass misread an ambiguous matplotlib SCATTER plot as 'upside down' and shipped a manifest pitch=pi 'fix' - this was WRONG and actually broke a model that was already correctly oriented, confirmed only after the live headless render probe screenshot showed the swapped-in model standing on its head (legs pointing at the sky). Re-diagnosed with a proper Poly3DCollection MESH render (not a scatter plot): the raw OBJ is ALREADY Y-up and standing correctly - legs planted at raw-y-min (~0.036), head/frill/nose-horn at raw-y-max (~6.99), tail trailing along Z. NO rotation needed; the pitch field was removed entirely. Also corrected the countershading direction to match: dark warm-brown RGB 0.42/0.30/0.20 at raw-y-max (head/dorsal end) fading to pale cream RGB 0.80/0.71/0.55 at raw-y-min (legs/ventral end) - the v1 GLB had this backwards too (dark at the legs, light at the head) as a direct consequence of the same misdiagnosis. Exported binary glTF (502,816 bytes, magic+JSON-chunk structurally validated, sha256 43628e1f2bb4a22193552e6a2e91e663f6f2d7bd8afb819bc23b19bdadb71c7e). Lesson for future fires: an ambiguous point-cloud scatter plot is NOT sufficient evidence for an orientation diagnosis - always render the actual triangulated mesh (Poly3DCollection or equivalent) before concluding a model needs a pitch/yaw correction, and always re-verify a pitch fix against a LIVE headless render screenshot of the swapped-in model before shipping, not just a Python-side simulation of the same (possibly wrong) premise.

OBJ (Aspose.3D Wavefront exporter, same creator workflow as dimetrodon/becklespinax/baryonyx/ichthyovenator/gorgonops) 2,872,465 bytes, byte-exact vs anonymous GraphQL print(id:764411){stls{fileSize}}, HTTP 200 anonymous download from the media.printables.com GCS bucket (no getDownloadLink mutation needed - the flat filePreviewPath-folder + lowercased-filename heuristic from fire237 worked directly). Rip-check clean (no daz/dinoraul/jurassic/turbosquid/cgtrader/franchise substrings; generic Object_4/Material.003 naming). Blender headless hangs at 0% CPU indefinitely on this machine (same infra issue independently re-confirmed on dakotaraptor + gorgonops) - converted via plain Python 3 + trimesh 4.12.2 (no Blender dependency): merged 22 print-optimized sub-parts (trunk, tusks, legs, ears, toenails - symmetric pairs) into one mesh via trimesh.load(force="mesh"), NO rotation needed - the raw OBJ was ALREADY Y-up with feet at y~0.02 (unlike the STL-sourced models from this creator, this OBJ export preserves a natural standing pose), recentered X/Z at origin, grounded min-Y at 0, height-based vertex-color countershading (dark reddish-brown fur RGB 0.32/0.20/0.11 at the back/hump fading to paler tan-brown RGB 0.55/0.42/0.28 at the legs/underside). No decimation needed (21,232 faces, already compact). Render-verified via 2 independent matplotlib Poly3DCollection silhouette renders (side profile Z-vs-Y, front view X-vs-Y): unmistakable woolly mammoth silhouette - domed head, curved tusks crossing in front, trunk hanging down, humped shoulder, short tail, 4 legs planted - correctly proportioned, not inverted, not foreshortened. Final GLB: 442,328 bytes, 11,654 verts / 21,232 faces. Creator 'Pan Wolodyjowski' (handle CHarbison_1036815) already has multiple clean CC-BY/CC-BY-SA vendored entries in this manifest (wendiceratops, pinacosaurus, panoplosaurus, etc) - not on the suspected-rip list; description "Odd yet famous Permian synapsid from Russia" + tags russian/permian/extinctanimals match the real animal's known profile, category 'Animals' (not Games/Movies) - no franchise-rip signal. Render-verified a second time on the final decimated+colored+scaled GLB (reloaded from disk): clean, correctly-proportioned, unmistakable Estemmenosuchus silhouette - bumpy warty skin, paired head-boss horns, stocky quadrupedal stance, short tail, countershading correct, feet at y=0, not inverted. Archetype for the mandatory G16 procedural first-paint fallback: reuses the existing ceratopsian/low-slung-quadruped procedural body pattern (bigHead=true, hornslike feature via small paired head bumps, tinyArms=false) - this glTF is the swapped-in higher-quality layer. dunkleosteus.notes: dinosaur-3d-discovery-loop-runbook LEAN one-off fire (2026-07-23). NEW candidate (not in prior candidate_backlog); sourced+built+shipped same fire per model-first doctrine. Found via printables.com/search/models?q=dunkleosteus (three named "Dunkleosteus" listings surfaced: id 342483 StormCrow13, id 1543972 and id 1610872 both by Brutus1717). License CONFIRMED CC-BY (id 1, "Creative Commons - Attribution") for id 342483 via anonymous GraphQL query{print(id:342483){license{id name}}}. Picked 342483 over the other two same-named listings: 342483's STL files are actually named 'dunkleosteous.stl'/'dunkleosteous_base.stl' (matches the subject) with a plausible personal-creator description ("I wanted a dunkleosteus mini so I made one", Patreon link, no franchise/game tokens) - REJECTED the two Brutus1717 listings as suspicious: their single STL files are named 'Frantic Fulffy-2.stl' and 'single_color-5.stl' respectively, generic filenames with ZERO textual connection to Dunkleosteus, a mislabel/rip-farm signature (a genuine creator's own file is very unlikely to be named unrelated to the subject) even though both are also nominally CC-BY. Used the primary 'dunkleosteous.stl' (not '_base', which is the separate print pedestal/stand file per print convention - the 'dunkleosteous.stl' geometry is the fish alone, confirmed by trimesh connected-component + multi-angle triangulated Poly3DCollection render, no pedestal disc present in this file). ORIENTATION: raw STL frame is Z-up (dorsal-ventral) with Y as the head-tail length axis (extent Y=106.6 >> X=40, Z=35.7; head-end located via the eyeball micro-components clustered near Y=-27, matching the head visible in the official Printables preview thumbnail) and X as left-right width - NOT already Y-up unlike estemmenosuchus. Applied a proper rotation (x,y,z)->(x,z,-y) [rotate -90 deg about X: converts old Z-up to new Y-up, old Y-length becomes new Z-length] BAKED into the exported geometry (no manifest pitch/yaw needed). Verified visually before AND after the rotation via multi-angle Poly3DCollection renders: post-rotation side-profile (elev=0) shows a level-swimming fish silhouette - boxy armored head with open jaw at one end, tapering tail with dorsal/tail fin at the other, pectoral fins hanging below the body, not inverted, not foreshortened, not standing on end. Converted via Python 3 + open3d 0.19.0 + trimesh 4.12.2 (blender binary present on this machine but not used, matching the proven open3d/trimesh pipeline from estemmenosuchus) - remove_duplicated_vertices / remove_degenerate_triangles / remove_unreferenced_vertices, simplify_quadric_decimation 120,026 -> 12,000 triangles, recenter X/Z at origin + ground min-Y at 0 + scale so length (Z extent) = 12 scene units, fix_normals for consistent outward winding (mesh is watertight post-export, positive volume, confirming correct orientation), then per-vertex height-based countershading (dark slate-grey armor plating RGB 0.22/0.24/0.27 at the dorsal/high-Y end fading to pale bony underside RGB 0.62/0.58/0.50 at the ventral/low-Y end) + binary glTF export (241,000 bytes, gltf magic verified). Render-verified a second time on the final decimated+colored+scaled GLB reloaded from disk: clean, unmistakable Dunkleosteus silhouette in side profile, countershading correct, feet-equivalent (fin base) at y=0, not inverted. Archetype for the mandatory G16 procedural first-paint fallback: reuses the existing marine/aquatic streamlined-torpedo-body procedural pattern (bigHead=true for the oversized armored skull, no flippers/tailFluke flags since real Dunkleosteus had simple paired fins not reptile flippers) - this glTF is the swapped-in higher-quality layer. Figures for the page cite BOTH the traditional popularized estimate (up to ~8.79m/~4t, widely repeated in museum/documentary sources) AND the 2023 peer-reviewed reassessment (Engelman, Diversity journal, MDPI - a new body-length estimation method using arthrodire-shaped analogues instead of unrelated gars, since only head/thoracic armor commonly fossilizes - suggests a much smaller ~4.1m for most individuals) per the honesty-disclosure doctrine (same class as the Liopleurodon myth-correction case), rather than inventing a single number. WIRED to a page this same fire: full CMS/JSP/registry bundle authored + gates run + shipped to staging and mirrored to prod (see wrapper ledger for commit SHAs).
